copy_seed: The first AI win for a field-service business is not replacing the office. It is making sure a missed job call becomes a summary, owner alert, and callback task.
A proof call should leave evidence: call record, summary, owner alert, callback task, dashboard proof. If it does not, do not trust the tool.
Show the Calls, Contacts, and Tasks path from one missed call to one callback task.
Plumbing leads are often urgent. The useful intake is issue, active water risk, address context, and callback window.
No AC and no heat calls need urgency, system notes, service area, and a fast owner alert.
Record the owner view: caller problem, urgency, and next action in one screen.
Generic AI reception is crowded. Missed-call recovery is narrower and easier to prove after one call.
Storm and leak calls should not sit in voicemail. Capture timing, property details, and inspection priority.
Show the $197 Starter path and explain why the first sale is the proof loop.
SMIRK is built to make owners and staff more effective, not to threaten the team.
If 100 researched touches get zero qualified replies, rewrite the segment or message before spending more.
Show one trade page and explain how the intake changes by service type.
Field crews miss estimate and cleanup calls while outside. The callback task is the useful artifact.
Do not call it hands-off SaaS until payment, workspace access, dashboard, proof call, owner alert, and callback task all pass.
Show how SMIRK differs from broad receptionist tools and developer voice platforms.
Busy bays need repair issue, vehicle notes, urgency, and callback timing captured cleanly.
Paid testing is capped at $500 until revenue or strong buyer intent appears.
Show screenshots or a walkthrough that proves the call-to-task loop without exposing caller data.
If missed calls are still hitting voicemail during jobs, run one proof call and inspect the evidence.
The sprint is simple: one paid activation, ten qualified conversations, or a clean stop condition.
